chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe storage, handling, and warming of expressed breast milk for under-twos, including hygiene practices, storage durations, and methods to prevent contamination and nutrient loss.
learning_objectives: Understand safe storage durations for expressed breast milk at different temperatures.
Learn proper methods for warming and handling expressed breast milk to preserve nutrients and prevent contamination.
Recognize the importance of pump hygiene and clean storage practices.
Identify signs of spoiled breast milk and appropriate actions to ensure infant safety.
key_facts: Expressed breast milk can be stored for up to 4 hours at room temperature, 4 days in a refrigerator (≤4°C), and 6 months in a freezer.
Thaw breast milk in the refrigerator or warm water; avoid microwaving to prevent hot spots and nutrient loss.
Discard warmed milk left in a feeding bottle/cup after 2 hours due to bacterial contamination risks.
Wash pump parts with soap and safe water after each use, and air-dry completely to ensure hygiene.
Use more conservative storage limits for medically fragile infants or if milk was expressed in unclean conditions.
reference_quote: Proper storage and preparation of breast milk are essential to ensure its safety and nutritional value for infants. (Source: American Academy of Pediatrics HealthyChildren)
document_key_topics: Safe warming methods for breast milk
Hygiene practices for milk expression and storage
